title: Units can move multiple times per phase (game-breaking)
description: movesUsed is incremented in applyMove (server/src/game.ts:138) but never validated. A unit can receive multiple moveOrder messages in the same phase, each with a valid path length, and all succeed. This breaks the core A&A rule that each unit moves at most once per phase.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A unit that has already moved is rejected with a clear error when a second moveOrder targets it in the same phase
- [ ] movesUsed >= def.move check added inside the per-unit for loop in applyMove, before the path-length check
- [ ] Units that have NOT moved are unaffected
- [ ] movesUsed reset to 0 in collect phase continues to work (already implemented at line 315)
- [ ] Infantry (move=1) cannot move 1 space then move again; tank (move=2) cannot move 1 space then 1 more space in a second order

title: Quit/forfeit logic: player exits mid-game, others continue or win
description: When a player clicks the quit button mid-game, the server must: (1) mark them as quit in the DB, (2) broadcast a notification to remaining players, (3) if exactly 2 human players remain after quit, declare the remaining player the winner, (4) if more players remain, auto-advance the quit player's turns (skip with minimal action) so the game can continue.

title: SQLite persistence layer: schema + CRUD for full game state
description: The server currently holds all game state in memory — a restart loses everything. We need a SQLite database that stores the complete game state so games survive server restarts and players can reconnect. Schema must cover: games, players, game_players, territory_states, units, power_states, pending_purchases, battles, battle_units, battle_rounds, game_log, and saves/snapshots.

title: Retreat logic has operator precedence bug causing potential crash
description: In server/src/game.ts:186, the || operator has lower precedence than &&, causing TERRITORY_MAP[u.movedFrom!]?.neighbors.includes(retreatTo) to execute unconditionally for every unit even when u or u.movedFrom is undefined. The non-null assertion u.movedFrom! on an undefined value can produce undefined lookups.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Retreat validation is rewritten with explicit null guards before any property access
- [ ] If u is undefined or u.movedFrom is undefined, the retreat is rejected cleanly without crashing
- [ ] Valid retreat destinations: the movedFrom territory OR any neighbor of movedFrom
- [ ] Invalid retreat (enemy territory, unconnected territory) is rejected with "Invalid retreat destination."
- [ ] Retreat log message includes the destination territory name

title: Client movement preview uses hardcoded range and ignores unit domain
description: In client/src/Game.tsx:26, reachable territory set uses const maxMove = 4 regardless of which units are selected. Infantry (move=1) and bombers (move=6) show the same orange highlight. Additionally, sea units see land territories as reachable and vice versa. The server correctly rejects illegal moves but the UI misleads players and causes confusing rejection messages.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Reachable set uses minimum move stat among all selected units
- [ ] Land units only highlight land territory paths; sea units only highlight sea paths; air units highlight all
- [ ] Selecting a mixed land+sea unit group shows empty reachable set (server would reject anyway)
- [ ] When moveUnits is empty, reachable set is empty (no fallback to maxMove=4)
- [ ] Bomber (move=6) highlights significantly more territory than infantry (move=1)

title: Quit/save UI button + confirmation dialog in game header
description: Add a Quit button to the game header. Clicking it shows a confirmation dialog ('Are you sure? You will forfeit this game.'). Also add an auto-save indicator showing last save time. The quit action sends a quitGame WS message; the save is automatic after every server state change.

title: Eliminated powers are never auto-detected
description: powers[p].eliminated exists in the type and is read in advancePhase (skip logic) and the client (greyed-out row), but nothing sets it to true. A power that loses all land territories continues buying units it can never place. Add automatic detection after territory captures.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] After any territory capture in applyResolveBattle, checkElimination() runs
- [ ] checkElimination marks eliminated=true for any power with zero land territories
- [ ] A log entry is added: "{Power} has been eliminated!"
- [ ] Already-eliminated powers are skipped in checkElimination
- [ ] The eliminated flag causes the power to be skipped in turn rotation (already implemented in advancePhase — verify it still works)

title: Damaged ships have no visual indicator
description: hitsTaken is tracked on UnitStack (shared/src/types.ts:57) and reset in collect phase, but never rendered. A battleship with 1 hit looks identical to a fresh one. Players cannot apply the A&A rule of choosing which units absorb hits, and cannot tell ship health at a glance.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Any unit with hitsTaken >= 1 shows a red damage badge on the board map (Board.tsx renderStacks)
- [ ] The same unit in the Territory Inspector (Panel.tsx TerritoryInspector) shows a visual damage state (outline, icon, or label)
- [ ] A fresh unit (hitsTaken=0 or undefined) is visually identical to current behavior
- [ ] Damage indicator is visible at typical zoom levels without obscuring the unit type icon
- [ ] No new props or state are required — reads directly from UnitStack.hitsTaken

title: Retreat uses window.prompt() — replace with map-based click UI
description: In client/src/Panel.tsx:502, the Retreat button calls prompt("Retreat to which adjacent friendly territory? (type id)") which requires players to know internal territory ID strings. Replace with a map interaction: clicking Retreat enters a retreat-selection mode where valid destinations are highlighted on the board and the player clicks one.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] prompt() is completely removed from Panel.tsx
- [ ] A new retreatContext state { territory: string } | null is added to Game.tsx
- [ ] Retreat button calls onRetreatRequest(b.territory) instead of prompt
- [ ] When retreatContext is set, board shows highlighted valid retreat destinations
- [ ] Clicking a highlighted territory sends resolveBattle with retreat:true, retreatTo:id
- [ ] A legend overlay shows "Select retreat destination — click to retreat" while retreatContext is active
- [ ] Clicking origin territory again or pressing Escape cancels the retreat selection

title: Non-combat move allows entry into unowned neutral territories
description: In server/src/game.ts:128, the non-combat move check only blocks entry into enemy-allied territories. Unowned (neutral) land territories with owner===null pass the check and can be freely entered. A&A 1942 2E rules prohibit entering neutral territories at all.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Non-combat move into a land territory with owner===null returns "Cannot enter neutral territory."
- [ ] Non-combat move into enemy-owned territory still returns "Cannot non-combat into enemy territory."
- [ ] Non-combat move into friendly-owned territory continues to succeed
- [ ] Sea zones (no owner concept) are unaffected by this change
- [ ] Combat move into neutral territory is unaffected (not this ticket's scope)

title: Cap game log at 200 entries to prevent memory growth
description: GameState.log grows unbounded. After 50+ turns of detailed play it can contain thousands of entries. All entries are serialized on every WebSocket broadcast. The client only renders the last 80. Add a server-side trim to cap at 200 entries per turn.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A trimLog(state) helper is added to game.ts that slices log to last 200 entries when exceeded
- [ ] trimLog is called once per turn at the end of the collect phase in advancePhase
- [ ] Log entries from the current turn are never truncated (only old entries are dropped)
- [ ] Client log display (last 80) is unaffected

title: Remove unused pendingOrders field (dead code)
description: state.pendingOrders is written in applyMove (lines 141-143) and cleared in advancePhase (line 320) but never read anywhere. It adds serialization overhead to every WebSocket broadcast. Remove the field entirely.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] pendingOrders push removed from applyMove
- [ ] pendingOrders=[] clear removed from advancePhase
- [ ] pendingOrders field removed from GameState interface in shared/src/types.ts
- [ ] pendingOrders:[] initializer removed from newGame() in server/src/game.ts
- [ ] No remaining references to pendingOrders anywhere in the codebase (grep confirms)
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly after removal
